perturbation.original_text: 4.2 The Receiving Party shall inform the Recipi ent of the terms of this Agreement and shall ensure that the Recipient complies with the term s of this Agreement as if the Recipient were a party to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 The Receiving Party shall inform the Recipi ent of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and shall ensure that the Recipient complies with the term s of this Agreement as if the Recipient were a party to this Agreement', the Receiving Party is only required to inform the Recipient about the agreement, but not to enforce compliance. This weakens the protection of Confidential Information and creates a contradiction. Clause 4.3 empowers the Disclosing Party to require the Receiving Party to enforce obligations. However, with the omitted clause, the Receiving Party has no duty to ensure the Recipient's compliance to start with. In-text contradiction arises because clause 4.3 presumes an obligation which is missing in 4.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 The Receiving Party shall at the request of the Disclosing Party and at its own expense take such reasonable steps as th e Disclosing Party ma y require to enforce the obligations of the Reci pient under Clause 4.2 incl uding (where necessary) the institution of legal proceedings.
perturbation.justification: The original text of clause 4.2 placed a duty on the Receiving Party to not only inform the Recipient of the terms of the Agreement, but also to ensure that the Recipient complies with those terms. The changed text removes the obligation to ensure compliance. Clause 4.3 states that the Receiving Party shall at the request of the Disclosing Party enforce the obligations of the Recipient under Clause 4.2. Since the changed version of Clause 4.2 does not require the Receiving Party to ensure compliance by the Recipient, Clause 4.3 is rendered contradictory. Clause 4.3 presumes an obligation in Clause 4.2 (ensuring Recipient compliance) that no longer exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Each party undertakes within three business days of the receipt of a written request of the other party or on termination of this Agreement, at the option of the other party: (a) to return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its po ssession, custody or control that bear or incorporate any pa rt of the other pa rty's Confidential Information; or (b) to destroy by shredding or incineration all of the other pa rty's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its possession, custody or control which bear or inco rporate any part of the other party's Confidential Information and take reason able steps to expung e all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been ma de) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information. Any destruction of Confidential Information will be certified in writing to the other party by an authorised officer supervising it.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Each party undertakes within a reasonable time of the receipt of a written request of the other party or on termination of this Agreement, at the option of the other party: (a) to return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its po ssession, custody or control that bear or incorporate any pa rt of the other pa rty's Confidential Information; or (b) to destroy all of the other pa rty's Confidential Information. Any destruction of Confidential Information will be certified in writing to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'within three business days' to 'within a reasonable time', we create ambiguity regarding the deadline. By removing the instruction 'by shredding or incineration', 'that part of all documents and other material in its possession, custody or control which bear or inco rporate any part of the other party's Confidential Information and take reason able steps to expung e all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been ma de) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information' and 'by an authorised officer supervising it' we are omitting essential steps on the destruction of the documents, by ommitting 'shredding or incineration' it makes it vague on how the document should be destroyed. In text contradiction created with clause 3 and clause 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall, in relation to the Conf idential Information of the other party, exercise in relation to the Confidential Information no lesser security measures and degree of care than those wh ich it applies to its own confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'within three business days' and 'shredding or incineration', providing clear instructions for returning or destroying confidential information. The changed text replaces 'within three business days' with 'within a reasonable time', creating ambiguity. It also removes the specific method of destruction ('shredding or incineration') and the requirement for certification by an authorized officer supervising it. This change contradicts the contradicted text, which states that each party should exercise no lesser security measures and degree of care than those which it applies to its own confidential information, due to the ambiguity of the deadline and the lack of specific destruction methods introduced by the changed text. Thus the omission creates an in-text contradiction, because it introduces uncertainty and a lower standard of care in the handling of confidential information. This adheres to the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10.4 It is acknowledged that damages would no t be an adequate remedy for a breach of this Agreement and each party is entitled to the re medy of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for a threatened or actual breach of this Agreement. Any claim for lo sses under this Agreement sha ll be restricted to direct loss only and shall not extend to indirect or cons equential loss.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.4 It is acknowledged that damages would no t be an adequate remedy for a breach of this Agreement and each party is entitled to the re medy of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for a threatened or actual breach of this Agreement. Any claim for lo sses under this Agreement sha ll not extend to indirect or cons equential loss.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'be restricted to direct loss only and', we permit claims for direct losses, however in the absense of that phrase it opens the door for claims regarding indirect and consequential losses creating uncertainty and therefore a possible in-text contradiction. This could be argued to contradict other clauses aiming for clarity and defined responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.3 The rights and remedies co ntained in this Agreemen t are cumulative and not exclusive of rights or remedies provided by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted loss claims to direct loss only. The changed text removes 'be restricted to direct loss only and', thereby broadening the scope of potential claims to include indirect and consequential losses. This directly contradicts the intent of clause 10.4 as it was originally written which aimed to limit claims to direct losses. While 10.3 states that remedies are cumulative, the removal of the restriction creates a conflict regarding the scope of recoverable losses within 10.4 itself making it an in-text contradiction as defined by Omissions.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2 In the absence of evidence of earlier r eceipt, any notice or other communication shall be deemed to ha ve been duly given, if delivered personally , when left at the address referred to in Clause 0 and if sent by fax, on completion of its transmission.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2 In the absence of evidence of earlier r eceipt, any notice or other communication shall be deemed to ha ve been duly given, if delivered personally , when left at the address referred to in Clause 11.1 and if sent by email, upon confirmation of transmission.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'fax' to 'email' and 'on completion of its transmission' to 'upon confirmation of transmission,' and introducing a condition on confirmation of transmission, which is not standard in the original Clause and not clear, we introduce a contradiction to where notices should be delivered as it could be easily missed and unenforced. This also contradicts Section 11.1, which specifies delivery by fax, creating an internal inconsistency. Intext contradiction is being created by not adding what should happen on those cases where an email confirmation is missing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 A notice or other communication under or in connection with th is Agreement shall be in writing, in Eng lish and shall be delive red personally or sent by fax, to the party due to receive the no tice or communication at its address set out in this Agreement or another address as specified by that party by wr itten notice to the other.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'fax' to 'email' and 'on completion of its transmission' to 'upon confirmation of transmission' introduces an inconsistency with Section 11.1, which explicitly states that notices should be sent by fax. By introducing email as an alternative delivery method with a new condition ('upon confirmation of transmission') without specifying what happens when confirmation is missing, the changed text contradicts the original agreement. The change impacts enforcability and creates confusion due to the explicit instruction for delivery by fax in section 11.1.
